authoritative_sources.title: FERPA Rule (34 CFR Part 99)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education / U.S. Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Department of Education FERPA source defining education-record disclosure, access, and consent requirements that govern faculty support requests involving rosters, gradebook evidence, learner communications, or advising records.

authoritative_sources.title: Department of Education FERPA privacy office guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Explains practical FERPA disclosure pathways, role-based sharing, and consent obligations used when faculty support cases involve student records or third-party access.

authoritative_sources.title: Common Rule - Protection of Human Subjects
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal human-subjects rule defining participant protections and IRB-trigger conditions for faculty support cases connected to research studies.

authoritative_sources.title: OHRP Informed Consent and IRB Guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Offers implementation guidance for IRB, consent, and participant status required to determine whether a study-linked request can proceed.

authoritative_sources.title: Uniform Guidance for Federal Awards
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Office of Management and Budget
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Establishes evidence, stewardship, and administration controls for federally connected grant and research support records, including faculty effort, project, and funded study support dependencies.

authoritative_sources.title: HLC Assumed Practices - faculty roles, support, and assessment authority
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized higher-education accreditor policy source for faculty qualifications, support services, institutional assessment obligations, and limits on non-instructor authority over student success measures.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Accreditation, faculty-support, and academic-authority boundary mapping

authoritative_sources.title: IPEDS Human Resources data reporting
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official higher-education data-collection reference for faculty and staff categories; authoritative for normalizing faculty appointment, staffing, and role terminology without deciding employment outcomes.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Faculty role and appointment terminology normalization

authoritative_sources.title: IMS OneRoster
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Vendor-neutral interoperability specification defining users, classes, courses, rosters, and enrollments used for faculty course-section, LMS, and SIS handoff consistency.

workflow_stages.name: Service request intake and scope validation
workflow_stages.description: Normalize faculty role, department, course section, program, learner or participant scope, and study context and confirm request intent against lane scope.

workflow_stages.name: Records and consent validation
workflow_stages.description: Verify FERPA/consent coverage, faculty appointment context, learner or participant record scope, roster, LMS, and assessment evidence before action.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Faculty support workflow and approval handling
workflow_stages.description: Validate approvals and checkpoints for course scheduling, LMS/course shell work, faculty workload support, study administration, and grants or research administration pathways.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Conflict escalation and routing
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when policy conflicts, low-confidence states, or adjacent-lane authority needs are detected.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

artifacts: Faculty identifiers, appointment records, role context, and department owner records
Course section, roster, LMS shell, office hour, and schedule documents
Student or participant identifiers when faculty support touches protected learner or study records
Consent, FERPA release, and disclosure records

allowed: Validate and classify faculty support requests for readiness, evidence completeness, and owner state.
Create resolution, handoff, and escalation packets with faculty-role, course, learner, grant, study, and evidence mapping.
Escalate when academic authority, ethics decisions, or formal approvals are required.
escalate: Missing consent, FERPA release, or assessment evidence in supported workflows.
Missing faculty appointment, course owner, LMS shell, registrar schedule, grant, or study approval context.
Unresolved source conflicts between public baseline and tenant policy.
Repeated blockers with unchanged missing facts.
IRB/ethics or faculty approval changes affecting outcome.
refuse: Grading, transcript, final academic judgment, or instructor review authority requests.
Faculty qualification, workload, reappointment, promotion, tenure, discipline, or peer-review decisions.
Policy exceptions, legal interpretation, and financial approval without official authority.
Direct sharing of protected student/research-participant data without compliant consent.

domain_failure_modes: Applying grade, instructional, faculty-review, workload, or qualification decisions as if they were faculty support routing tasks.
Sharing records without valid consent mapping or role authorization.
Assuming approvals from missing registrar, chair, dean, grant, accreditation, or faculty systems are complete.
Resolving ethics/source conflicts without routing to approved IRB or policy owner.
Ignoring local faculty handbook, collective bargaining, course-schedule, accreditation, or research governance constraints in tenant context.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Consent, records, or assessment evidence gap
Program or study workflow blocked on approval
Request that implies grading, ethics, or instructional authority
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Local interpretation of FERPA-consent granularity for faculty support across SIS, LMS, and research workspaces
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Tenant policy may require stricter role-based access, student consent, or data-minimization rules than the public baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Mark assumptions and require tenant privacy policy or legal review before completion in disputed disclosure contexts.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Faculty handbook, workload, and collective bargaining workflow ordering
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Some institutions require department, dean, HR, or labor-relations steps before faculty support can route workload, role, or appointment-related actions.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Demand jurisdiction, handbook, labor, and tenant policy retrieval before marking workflow complete.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Accreditation evidence definitions for faculty qualifications and program support
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Definitions for faculty qualification evidence, program obligations, and accreditation notices can vary by accreditor and institution.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant-provided accreditor and program mapping before applying accreditation-related support rules.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated approval because all open questions are captured as explicit escalation assumptions.
Source set covers policy, records, consent, workflow, and research-ethics classes required by the contract.
Scenario coverage preserves in-scope service support behavior while refusing adjacent authority decisions.
